## reference · ChatGPT Google AI Overviews Perplexity
* Google AI Overviews appear in roughly 50% of all searches.
* ChatGPT handles over 200 million weekly active users.
* Pages cited in AI Overviews earn 35% more organic clicks than non-cited competitors.
* Visitors arriving from Perplexity convert at roughly 11 times the rate of traditional organic search traffic.
* Only 11% of domains are cited by both ChatGPT and Perplexity.
* ChatGPT cites brands just 0.59% of the time, while Perplexity cites brands at 13.05%.
* Wikipedia is the #1 cited source for ChatGPT (7.8% of all citations).
* Reddit is the #1 cited source for Google AI Overviews (2.2%) and Perplexity (6.6%).
* Optimizing for "AI search" as a single category is ineffective due to different mechanics and winning strategies for each platform.
* ChatGPT's citation process is highly selective, favoring content with structured H1/H2/H3 headings, direct-answer formatting, FAQ schema, and cited claims.
* Domains with active profiles on platforms like G2 or Capterra show 3 times higher citation probability than sites without such presence.
* Brand mentions across YouTube and broader web properties are among the strongest correlating factors for ChatGPT AI brand visibility.
* Approximately 29% of ChatGPT's citations reference content published in 2022 or earlier.
* Google AI Overviews' citation selection is determined by 7 factors, including semantic completeness, multi-modal content integration, and real-time factual verification.
* Semantic completeness is the strongest predictor of AI Overview citation selection, with a correlation of r=0.87.
* Multi-modal content integration shows 156% higher selection rates versus text-only content.
* Structured data markup contributes a 73% selection rate improvement over unstructured pages.
* Perplexity performs a real-time web search for every single query, drawing from multiple search APIs including Google and Bing.
* Perplexity favors pages with structured H2/H3 headings, visible statistics, named sources, and content that cites other authoritative sources.
* Reddit accounts for 46.7% of Perplexity's top citation sources.
* Earned media has a near-real-time path to Perplexity citations.
* Perplexity's user base skews toward journalists, analysts, investors, and technical professionals, resulting in high-converting traffic.
* LLM referral traffic converts at 1.66% for signups compared to 0.15% from traditional organic search.
* Despite accounting for only 15-20% of AI referral volume, Perplexity delivers the highest ROI per citation earned of any platform.
* Universal citation signals that work across all three platforms include semantic completeness, E-E-A-T signals, structured data markup, original data, and earned media distribution.
* Pages with strong E-E-A-T indicators show 22% higher visibility in AI-generated results.
* Content freshness is part of E-E-A-T, with 53% of content cited in AI search having been updated within the last six months.
* FAQ schema alone correlates with 40% higher citation weighting in ChatGPT and significantly higher citation rates in Google AI Overviews.
* Including original research, proprietary statistics, aggregated client results, or survey data makes content a citation magnet.
* Distributing content to a wide range of publications can increase AI citations by up to 325% compared to publishing only on your own site.
* The first 30% of a page accounts for 44.2% of all LLM citations, making it crucial to put important facts and direct answers at the top of the page.
Sources: https://www.leapd.ai/blog/ai-visibility/how-chatgpt-google-ai-overviews-and-perplexity-source-information-in-2026
